methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Frame.recenter
documentation:
Recenter the frame using the satellite spots or a radon transform.

Parameters
----------
method : {'satspots', 'radon'}, str optional
    Method for recentering the frame.
xy : tuple, optional
    Tuple with coordinates X,Y of the satellite spots. When the spots
    are in an X configuration, the order is the following: top-left,
    top-right, bottom-left and bottom-right. When the spots are in an
    + (cross-like) configuration, the order is the following: top,
    right, left, bottom.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Frame.rescale
documentation:
Resample the image (upscaling or downscaling).

Parameters
----------
scale : int, float or tuple
    Scale factor for upsampling or downsampling the frames in the cube.
    If a tuple it corresponds to the scale along x and y.
imlib : {'ndimage', 'opencv', 'vip-fft'}, str optional
    Library used for image transformations. ndimage is the default.
interpolation : str, optional
    For 'ndimage' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
    'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation
    is the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is
    the worst option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
    For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
    The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
    slowest and accurate.
verbose : bool, optional
    Whether to print out additional info such as the new image shape.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Frame.rotate
documentation:
Rotate the image by a given `angle`.

# Parameters

imlib

: Library used for image transformations. Opencv is faster than
  ndimage or skimage.

interpolation

: For 'skimage' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
  'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation
  is the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is
  the poorer option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
  For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
  The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
  slowest and accurate. 'lanczos4' is the default.

cxy

: Coordinates X,Y of the point with respect to which the rotation
  will be performed. By default the rotation is done with respect to
  the center of the frames, as it is returned by the function
  vip_hci.var.frame_center.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Frame.shift
documentation:
Shift the image.

Parameters
----------
shift_y, shift_x: float
    Shifts in x and y directions.
imlib : {'opencv', 'ndimage-fourier', 'ndimage-interp', 'vip-fft'}, str opt
    Library or method used for performing the image shift.
    'ndimage-fourier', does a fourier shift operation and preserves
    better the pixel values (therefore the flux and photometry).
    Interpolation based shift ('opencv' and 'ndimage-interp') is faster
    than the fourier shift. 'opencv' is recommended when speed is
    critical.
interpolation : {'bicubic', 'bilinear', 'nearneig'}, optional
    Only used in case of imlib is set to 'opencv' or 'ndimage-interp',
    where the images are shifted via interpolation.
    For 'ndimage-interp' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
    'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation is
    the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is the
    poorer option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
    For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
    The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
    slowest and accurate. 'lanczos4' is the default.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Frame.snr
documentation:
Calculate the S/N for a test resolution element `source_xy`.

# Parameters

source_xy

: X and Y coordinates of the planet or test speckle.

plot

: Plots the frame and the apertures considered for clarity.

verbose

: Chooses whether to print some output or not.

# Returns

snr_val

: Value of the S/N for `source_xy`.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Frame.stats
documentation:
Calculate statistics on the image, both in the full-frame and in a region.

The region can be a circular aperture or an annulus. Also, the S/N of the either
`source_xy` or the max pixel is calculated.

# Parameters

region

: Region in which basic statistics (mean, stddev, median and max) are
  calculated.

radius

: Radius of the circular aperture.

xy

: Center of the circular aperture.

annulus_inner_radius

: Inner radius of the annular region.

annulus_width

: Width of the annular region.

source_xy

: Coordinates for which the S/N information will be obtained. If None,
  the S/N is estimated for the pixel with the maximum value.

verbose

: Whether to print out the values of the calculated statistics.

plot

: Whether to plot the frame, histograms and region.

High-contrast imaging frame (2d array).

# Parameters

data

: 2d array.

hdu

: If `cube` is a String, `hdu` indicates the HDU from the FITS file.
  By default the first HDU is used.

fwhm

: The FWHM associated with this dataset (instrument dependent). Required
  for several methods (operations on the cube).

classes.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.__init__
documentation: Initialize the Dataset object.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.collapse
documentation: Collapsing the sequence into a 2d array.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.crop_frames
documentation:
Crop the frames of the sequence (3d or 4d cube).

# Parameters

size

: New size of the (square) frames.

xy

: X, Y coordinates of new frame center. If you are getting the
  coordinates from ds9 subtract 1, python has 0-based indexing.

force

: `Size` and the original size of the frames must be both even or
  odd. With `force` set to True this condition can be avoided.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.derotate
documentation:
Derotating the frames of the sequence according to the parallactic angles.

# Parameters

imlib

: Library used for image transformations. Opencv is faster than
  ndimage or skimage.

interpolation

: For 'skimage' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
  'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation
  is the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is
  the poorer option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
  For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
  The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
  slowest and accurate. 'lanczos4' is the default.

cxy

: Coordinates X,Y of the point with respect to which the rotation
  will be performed. By default the rotation is done with respect to
  the center of the frames, as it is returned by the function
  vip_hci.var.frame_center.

nproc

: Whether to rotate the frames in the sequence in a multi-processing
  fashion. Only useful if the cube is significantly large (frame size
  and number of frames).

border_mode

: See the documentation of the `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate`
  function.

mask_val

: See the documentation of the `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate`
  function.

edge_blend

: See the documentation of the `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate`
  function.

interp_zeros

: See the documentation of the `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate`
  function.

ker: int, optional

: See the documentation of the `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate`
  function.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.drop_frames
documentation:
Slice the cube so that all frames between ``` n``and ``m ``` are kept.

The indices `n` and `m` are included and 1-based.

# Examples

For a cube which has 5 frames numbered `1, 2, 3, 4, 5`, calling
`ds.drop_frames(2, 4)` would result in the frames `2, 3, 4` to be
kept, so the first and the last frame would be discarded.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.filter
documentation:
High/low pass filtering the frames of the cube.

# Parameters

method

: Low-pass or high-pass filtering.

mode

: Type of low/high-pass filtering.
  `median`

  > [lp] applies a median low-pass filter to the image.

  `gauss`

  : [lp] applies a Gaussian low-pass filter to the image.

  `laplacian`

  : [hp] applies a Laplacian filter with kernel size defined by
    `kernel_size` using the Opencv library.

  `laplacian-conv`

  : [hp] applies a Laplacian high-pass filter by defining a kernel (with
    `kernel_size`) and using the `convolve_fft` Astropy function.

  `median-subt`

  : [hp] subtracts a median low-pass filtered version of the image.

  `gauss-subt`

  : [hp] subtracts a Gaussian low-pass filtered version of the image.

  `fourier-butter`

  : [hp] applies a high-pass 2D Butterworth filter in Fourier domain.

  `hann`

  : [hp] uses a Hann window.

median_size

: Size of the median box for the `median` or `median-subt` filter.

kernel_size

: Size of the Laplacian kernel used in `laplacian` mode. It must be
  a positive odd integer value.

fwhm_size

: Size of the Gaussian kernel used in `gauss` or `gauss-subt` mode.

btw_cutoff

: Frequency cutoff for low-pass 2d Butterworth filter used in
  `fourier-butter` mode.

btw_order

: Order of low-pass 2d Butterworth filter used in `fourier-butter` mode.

hann_cutoff

: Frequency cutoff for the `hann` mode.

gauss_mode

: 'conv' uses the multidimensional gaussian filter from scipy.ndimage and
  'convfft' uses the fft convolution with a 2d Gaussian kernel.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.frame_distances
documentation:
Calculate the frame distance/correlation with respect to a reference image.

Parameters
----------
frame : int or 2d array
    Reference frame in the cube or 2d array.
region : {'full', 'annulus'}, string optional
    Whether to use the full frames or a centered annulus.
dist : {'sad','euclidean','mse','pearson','spearman', 'ssim'}, str optional
    Which criterion to use.
inner_radius : None or int, optional
    The inner radius when mode is 'annulus'.
width : None or int, optional
    The width when mode is 'annulus'.
plot : bool, optional
    Whether to plot the distances or not.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.frame_stats
documentation:
Calculate statistics on a `region` of each image of the sequence.

The `region` can be a circular aperture or an annulus.

# Parameters

region

: Region in which basic statistics (mean, stddev, median and max) are
  calculated.

radius

: Radius of the circular aperture.

xy

: Center of the circular aperture.

annulus_inner_radius

: Inner radius of the annular region.

annulus_width

: Width of the annular region.

wavelength

: Index of the wavelength to be analyzed in the case of a 4d cube.

plot

: Whether to plot the frame, histograms and region.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.inject_companions
documentation:
Inject fake companions in 3d or 4d cubes.

# Parameters

flux

: Factor for controlling the brightness of the fake companions.

rad_dists

: Vector of radial distances of fake companions in pixels.

n_branches

: Number of azimutal branches.

theta

: Angle in degrees for rotating the position of the first branch that
  by default is located at zero degrees. Theta counts
  counterclockwise from the positive x axis.

imlib

: Library or method used for performing the image shift.
  'ndimage-fourier', does a fourier shift operation and preserves
  better the pixel values (therefore the flux and photometry).
  Interpolation based shift ('opencv' and 'ndimage-interp') is faster
  than the fourier shift. 'opencv' is recommended when speed is
  critical.

interpolation

: Only used in case of imlib is set to 'opencv' or 'ndimage-interp',
  where the images are shifted via interpolation. For 'ndimage-interp'
  library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic', 'bicubic',
  'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation is the
  fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is the
  poorer option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
  For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
  The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
  slowest and accurate. 'lanczos4' is the default.

full_output

: Return the coordinates of the injected companions.

verbose

: If True prints out additional information.

# Returns

yx

: [full_output=True] Pixel coordinates of the injections in the first
  frame (and first wavelength for 4D cubes). These are only the new
  injections - all injections (from multiple calls to this function)
  are stored in `self.injections_yx`.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.generate_copies_with_injections
documentation:
Create copies of this dataset, containing different random injections.

# Parameters

n_copies

: This is the number of 'cube copies' returned.

inrad,outrad

: Inner and outer radius of the injections. The actual injection
  position is chosen randomly.

dist_flux

: Tuple describing the flux selection. Method can be a function, the
  `*params` are passed to it. Method can also be a string, for a
  pre-defined random function:

  > `('skewnormal', skew, mean, var)`
  >
  > : uses scipy.stats.skewnorm.rvs
  >
  > `('uniform', low, high)`
  >
  > : uses np.random.uniform
  >
  > `('normal', loc, scale)`
  >
  > : uses np.random.normal

# Yields

fake_dataset

: Copy of the original Dataset, with injected companions.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.get_nbytes
documentation: Return the total number of bytes the Dataset consumes.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.copy
documentation:
Create an in-memory copy of this Dataset.

This is especially useful for keeping a backup copy of the original
dataset before modifying if (e.g. with injections).

# Parameters

deep

: By default, a deep copy is created. That means every (sub)attribute
  is copied in memory. While this requires more memory, one can safely
  modify the attributes without touching the original Dataset. When
  `deep=False`, a shallow copy of the Dataset is returned
  instead. That means all attributes (e.g. `self.cube`) point back
  to the original object's attributes. Pay attention when modifying
  such a shallow copy!

check_mem

: [deep=True] If True, verifies that the system has enough memory to
  store the result.

# Returns

new_dataset

: (deep) copy of this Dataset.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.load_angles
documentation:
Load the PA vector from a FITS file. It is possible to specify the HDU.

# Parameters

angles

: List or vector with the parallactic angles.

hdu

: If `angles` is a String, `hdu` indicates the HDU from the FITS
  file. By default the first HDU is used.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.load_wavelengths
documentation:
Load the scaling factors vector from a FITS file.

It is possible to specify the HDU.

# Parameters

wavelengths

: List or vector with the wavelengths.

hdu

: If `wavelengths` is a String, `hdu` indicates the HDU from the
  FITS file. By default the first HDU is used.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.mask_center
documentation:
Mask the values inside/outside a centered circular aperture.

# Parameters

radius

: Radius of the circular aperture.

fillwith

: Value to put instead of the masked out pixels.

mode

: When set to 'in' then the pixels inside the radius are set to
  `fillwith`. When set to 'out' the pixels outside the circular
  mask are set to `fillwith`.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.normalize_psf
documentation:
Normalize a PSF (2d or 3d array), to have the flux in a 1xFWHM aperture = 1.

Also allows to crop the array and center the PSF at the center of the frame(s).

# Parameters

fit_fwhm: bool, optional

: Whether to fit a `model` to estimate the FWHM instead of using the
  self.fwhm attribute.

size

: If int it will correspond to the size of the squared subimage to be
  cropped form the psf array.

threshold

: Sets to zero small values, trying to leave only the core of the PSF.

mask_core

: Sets the radius of a circular aperture for the core of the PSF,
  everything else will be set to zero.

imlib

: Library or method used for performing the image shift.
  'ndimage-fourier', does a fourier shift operation and preserves
  better the pixel values (therefore the flux and photometry).
  Interpolation based shift ('opencv' and 'ndimage-interp') is faster
  than the fourier shift. 'opencv' is recommended when speed is
  critical.

interpolation

: Only used in case of imlib is set to 'opencv' or 'ndimage-interp',
  where the images are shifted via interpolation. For 'ndimage-interp'
  library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic', 'bicubic',
  'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation is the
  fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is the
  poorer option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
  For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
  The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
  slowest and accurate. 'lanczos4' is the default.

force_odd

: If True the resulting array will have odd size (and the PSF will be
  placed at its center). If False, and the frame size is even, then
  the PSF will be put at the center of an even-sized frame.

verbose

: If True intermediate results are printed out.
TODO: change fit_fwhm description (include float support)

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.plot
documentation:
Plot the frames of a 3D or 4d cube.

# Parameters

kwargs

: Parameters passed to the function `plot_cubes` of the package
  `HCIplot`.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.recenter
documentation:
Recenter frame-to-frame.

# Parameters

method

: Recentering method.

xy

: For the 2dfitting, `xy` are the coordinates of the center of the
  subimage (wrt the original frame). For the satellite spots method,
  it is a tuple with coordinates X,Y of the 4 satellite spots. When
  the spots are in an X configuration, the order is the following:
  top-left, top-right, bottom-left and bottom-right. When the spots
  are in an + (cross-like) configuration, the order is the following:
  top, right, left, bottom.

subi_size

: Size of the square subimage sides in pixels.

model

: [method=2dfit] Sets the type of fit to be used.
  'gauss' for a 2d Gaussian fit and 'moff' for a 2d Moffat fit.

nproc

: Number of processes (>1) for parallel computing. If 1 then it runs
  in serial. If None the number of processes will be set to
  (cpu_count()/2).

imlib

: Library or method used for performing the image shift.
  'ndimage-fourier', does a fourier shift operation and preserves
  better the pixel values (therefore the flux and photometry).
  Interpolation based shift ('opencv' and 'ndimage-interp') is faster
  than the fourier shift. 'opencv' is recommended when speed is
  critical.

interpolation

: Only used in case of imlib is set to 'opencv' or 'ndimage-interp',
  where the images are shifted via interpolation.
  For 'ndimage-interp' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
  'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation is
  the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is the
  poorer option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
  For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
  The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
  slowest and accurate. 'lanczos4' is the default.

offset

: [method=2dfit] If None the region of the frames
  used for the 2d Gaussian/Moffat fit is shifted to the center of the
  images (2d arrays). If a tuple is given it serves as the offset of
  the fitted area wrt the center of the 2d arrays.

negative

: [method=2dfit/dftups/dftupspeckles] If True a negative 2d
  Gaussian/Moffat fit is performed.

threshold

: [method=2dfit] If True the background pixels
  (estimated using sigma clipped statistics) will be replaced by
  small random Gaussian noise.

save_shifts

: [method=2dfit/dftups] Whether to save the shifts to a file in disk.

cy_1, cx_1

: [method=dftups] Coordinates of the center of the
  subimage for fitting a 2d Gaussian and centroiding the 1st frame.

upsample_factor

: [method=dftups] Upsampling factor (default 100).
  Images will be registered to within 1/upsample_factor of a pixel.

alignment_iter

: [method=dftupspeckles] Number of alignment
  iterations (recomputes median after each iteration).

gamma

: [method=dftupspeckles] Applies a gamma correction
  to emphasize speckles (useful for faint stars).

min_spat_freq

: [method=dftupspeckles] Spatial frequency for high
  pass filter.

max_spat_freq

: [method=dftupspeckles] Spatial frequency for low
  pass filter.

recenter_median

: [method=dftupspeckles] Recenter the frames at each
  iteration based on the gaussian fit.

sigfactor

: [method=satspots] The background pixels will
  be thresholded before fitting a 2d Gaussian to the data using sigma
  clipped statistics. All values smaller than (MEDIAN +
  sigfactor\*STDDEV) will be replaced by small random Gaussian noise.

cropsize

: [method=radon] Size in pixels of the cropped central area of the
  input array that will be used. It should be large enough to contain
  the satellite spots.

hsize

: [method=radon] Size of the box for the grid search. The frame is
  shifted to each direction from the center in a hsize length with a
  given step.

step

: [method=radon] The step of the coordinates change.

mask_center

: [method=radon] If None the central area of the frame is kept. If int
  a centered zero mask will be applied to the frame. By default the
  center isn't masked.

verbose

: Whether to print to stdout the timing and aditional info.

debug

: If True debug information is printed and plotted.

plot

: Whether to plot the shifts.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.remove_badframes
documentation:
Find outlying/bad frames and slice the cube accordingly.

Besides modifying `self.cube` and `self.angles`, also sets a
`self.good_indices` which contain the indices of the angles which were
kept.

# Parameters

method

: Method which is used to determine bad frames. Refer to the
  `preproc.badframes` submodule for explanation of the different
  methods.

frame_ref

: [method=corr] Index of the frame that will be used as a reference or
  2d reference array.

crop_size

: [method=corr] Size in pixels of the square subframe to be analyzed.

dist

: [method=corr] One of the similarity or dissimilarity measures from
  function vip_hci.stats.distances.cube_distance().

percentile

: [method=corr] The percentage of frames that will be discarded
  [0..100].

stat_region

: [method=pxstats] Whether to take the statistics from a circle or an
  annulus.

inner_radius

: [method=pxstats] If stat_region is 'annulus' then 'in_radius' is the
  inner radius of the annular region. If stat_region is 'circle' then
  'in_radius' is the radius of the aperture.

width

: [method=pxstats] Size of the annulus. Ignored if mode is 'circle'.

top_sigma

: [method=pxstats] Top boundary for rejection.

low_sigma

: [method=pxstats] Lower boundary for rejection.

window

: [method=pxstats] Window for smoothing the median and getting the
  rejection statistic.

roundlo,roundhi

: [method=ellip] : Lower and higher bounds for the ellipticipy.

lambda_ref

: [4D cube] Which wavelength to consider when determining bad frames
  on a 4D cube.

plot

: If true it plots the mean fluctuation as a function of the frames
  and the boundaries.

verbose

: Show debug output.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.rescale
documentation:
Resample the pixels (upscaling or downscaling the frames).

Parameters
----------
scale : int, float or tuple
    Scale factor for upsampling or downsampling the frames in the cube.
    If a tuple it corresponds to the scale along x and y.
imlib : {'ndimage', 'opencv', 'vip-fft'}, str optional
    Library used for image transformations. ndimage is the default.
interpolation : str, optional
    For 'ndimage' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
    'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation
    is the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is
    the worst option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
    For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
    The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
    slowest and accurate.
verbose : bool, optional
    Whether to print out additional info such as the new cube shape.

methods.name: vip_hci.objects.dataset.Dataset.subsample
documentation:
Sumb-sample temporally the sequence (3d or 4d cube).

Parameters
----------
window : int
    Window for mean/median.
mode : {'mean', 'median'}, optional
    Switch for choosing mean or median.

High-contrast imaging dataset class.

# Parameters

cube

: 3d or 4d high-contrast image sequence. If a string is provided, cube is
  interpreted as the path of the FITS file containing the sequence.

hdu

: If `cube` is a String, `hdu` indicates the HDU from the FITS file.
  By default the first HDU is used.

angles

: The vector of parallactic angles.

wavelengths

: The vector of wavelengths (to be used as scaling factors).

fwhm

: The FWHM associated with this dataset (instrument dependent). Required
  for several methods (operations on the cube).

px_scale

: The pixel scale associated with this dataset (instrument dependent).

psf

: The PSF template associated with this dataset.

psfn

: Normalized/cropped/centered version of the PSF template associated with
  this dataset.

cuberef

: 3d or 4d high-contrast image sequence. To be used as a reference cube.

modules.name: vip_hci.psfsub.loci
documentation:
Module with a frame differencing algorithm for ADI and ADI+mSDI post-processing.

[^cite_pue12]:
    Pueyo et al. 2012

    **Application of a Damped Locally Optimized Combination of Images Method to
    the Spectral Characterization of Faint Companions Using an Integral Field
    Spectrograph**

    *The Astrophysical Journal Supplements, Volume 199, p. 6*

    [https://arxiv.org/abs/1111.6102](https://arxiv.org/abs/1111.6102)
! /usr/bin/env python

functions.name: vip_hci.psfsub.loci.xloci
documentation:
Locally Optimized Combination of Images (LOCI) algorithm as in [^cite_laf07].
The PSF is modeled (for ADI and ADI+mSDI) with a least-square combination
of neighbouring frames (solving the equation a x = b by computing a vector
x of coefficients that minimizes the Euclidean 2-norm || b - a x ||^2).

This algorithm is also compatible with IFS data to perform LOCI-SDI, in a
similar fashion as suggested in [^cite_pue12] (albeit without dampening zones).

# Parameters

all_args: list, optional

: Positionnal arguments for the LOCI algorithm. Full list of parameters
  below.

all_kwargs: dictionary, optional

: Mix of keyword arguments that can initialize a LOCIParams and the optional
  'rot_options' dictionnary, with keyword values for "border_mode", "mask_val",
  "edge_blend", "interp_zeros", "ker" (see documentation of
  `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate`). Can also contain a LOCIParams named as
  `algo_params`.

# LOCI parameters

cube

: Input cube.

angle_list

: Vector of derotation angles to align North up in your cube images.

scale_list

: If provided, triggers mSDI reduction. These should be the scaling
  factors used to re-scale the spectral channels and align the speckles
  in case of IFS data (ADI+mSDI cube). Usually, these can be approximated
  by the last channel wavelength divided by the other wavelengths in the
  cube (more thorough approaches can be used to get the scaling factors,
  e.g. with `vip_hci.preproc.find_scal_vector`).

fwhm

: Size of the FWHM in pixels. Default is 4.

metric

: Distance metric to be used ('cityblock', 'cosine', 'euclidean', 'l1',
  'l2', 'manhattan', 'correlation', etc). It uses the scikit-learn
  function `sklearn.metrics.pairwise.pairwise_distances` (check its
  documentation).

dist_threshold

: Indices with a distance larger than `dist_threshold` percentile will
  initially discarded. 100 by default.

delta_rot

: Factor for adjusting the parallactic angle threshold, expressed in
  FWHM. Default is 1 (excludes 1 FWHM on each side of the considered
  frame). If a tuple of two floats is provided, they are used as the lower
  and upper intervals for the threshold (grows linearly as a function of
  the separation).

delta_sep

: The threshold separation in terms of the mean FWHM (for ADI+mSDI data).
  If a tuple of two values is provided, they are used as the lower and
  upper intervals for the threshold (grows as a function of the
  separation).

radius_int

: The radius of the innermost annulus. By default is 0, if >0 then the
  central circular region is discarded.

asize

: The size of the annuli, in pixels.

n_segments

: The number of segments for each annulus. When a single integer is given
  it is used for all annuli. When set to 'auto', the number of segments is
  automatically determined for every annulus, based on the annulus width.

nproc

: Number of processes for parallel computing. If None the number of
  processes will be set to cpu_count()/2. By default the algorithm works
  in single-process mode.

solver

: Choosing the solver of the least squares problem. `lstsq` uses the
  standard scipy least squares solver. `nnls` uses the scipy
  non-negative least-squares solver.

tol

: Valid when `solver` is set to lstsq. Sets the cutoff for 'small'
  singular values; used to determine effective rank of a. Singular values
  smaller than `tol * largest_singular_value` are considered zero.
  Smaller values of `tol` lead to smaller residuals (more aggressive
  subtraction).

optim_scale_fact

: If >1, the least-squares optimization is performed on a larger segment,
  similar to LOCI. The optimization segments share the same inner radius,
  mean angular position and angular width as their corresponding
  subtraction segments.

adimsdi

: Changes the way the 4d cubes (ADI+mSDI) are processed.

  `skipadi`: the multi-spectral frames are rescaled wrt the largest
  wavelength to align the speckles and the least-squares model is
  subtracted on each spectral cube separately.

  `double`: a first subtraction is done on the rescaled spectral frames
  (as in the `skipadi` case). Then the residuals are processed again in
  an ADI fashion.

imlib

: See the documentation of the `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate` function.

interpolation

: See the documentation of the `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate` function.

collapse

: Sets the way of collapsing the frames for producing a final image.

verbose: bool, optional

: If True prints info to stdout.

full_output: bool, optional

: Whether to return the final median combined image only or along with
  2 other residual cubes (before and after derotation).

# Returns

cube_res

: [full_output=True] Cube of residuals.

cube_der

: [full_output=True] Derotated cube of residuals.

frame_der_median

: Median combination of the de-rotated cube of residuals.

modules.name: vip_hci.psfsub.rollsub
documentation:
Implementation of a roll subtraction algorithm for PSF subtraction in imaging
sequences obtained with space-based instruments (e.g. JWST or HST) with
different roll angles. The concept was proposed in [^cite_sch98] for application to
HST/NICMOS observations.

[^cite_sch98]:
    Schneider et al. 1998

    Exploration of the environments of nearby stars with the NICMOS

    coronagraph: instrumental performance considerations\*\*
    | *Proc. SPIE Vol. 3356, pp. 222-233*
! /usr/bin/env python

functions.name: vip_hci.psfsub.rollsub.roll_sub
documentation:
Perform roll-subtraction, followed by derotation and stacking of residual images.

Reference: [^cite_sch98].

# Parameters

all_args: list, optional

: Positionnal arguments for the roll_sub algorithm. Full list of
  parameters below.

all_kwargs: dictionary, optional

: Mix of keyword arguments that can initialize a ROLL_SUB_Params and the
  optional `rot_options` dictionary (with keywords `border_mode`,
  `mask_val`, `edge_blend`, `interp_zeros`, `ker`; see docstrings
  of `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate`). Can also contain a
  ROLL_SUB_Params object/dictionary named `algo_params`.

# Parameters

cube

: Input cube. Can also be a 4d array, with images obtained with the 1st
  and 2nd roll angle values are provided in the 1st and 2nd dimension of
  the 4d cube, respectively.

angle_list

: Roll angles associated to each frame. Can also be a list/tuple of 2
  elements. In the latter case, if input cube is 3D, it will assume that
  the first half of the frames are associated to the first roll angle,
  and the second half to the second roll angle.

mode

: If `mode` is set to 'mean' or 'median', only the mean/median frame of
  the image sequence obtained at the first roll angle is subtracted to the
  mean/media image from the sequence obtained with the second roll angle,
  and vice-versa. If `mode` is set to 'individual' a pair-wise
  subtraction of individual images obtained at each roll angle is
  performed, following the same order as in the input cube. To work, this
  mode requires the same number of images obtained with each roll angle.

imlib

: See the documentation of `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate`.

interpolation

: See the documentation of the `vip_hci.preproc.frame_rotate` function.

collapse

: Sets how temporal residual frames should be combined to produce an
  ADI image.

fwhm_lp_bef

: FWHM of the Gaussian kernel used for low-pass filtering of the input
  images. Can be useful for mode='individual' and spatially subsampled
  input images. If set to 0 (default), no low-pass filtering is performed.

fwhm_lp_aft

: FWHM of the Gaussian kernel used for low-pass filtering of the final
  image. Can be useful for spatially subsampled input images. If set to 0
  (default), no low-pass filtering is performed.

nproc

: Number of processes for parallel computing. If None the number of
  processes will be set to cpu_count()/2. By default the algorithm works
  in single-process mode.

full_output: bool, optional

: Whether to return the final median combined image only or with other
  intermediate arrays.

verbose

: If True prints to stdout intermediate info.

# Returns

cube_res

: [full_output=True] The cube of residuals.

cube_der

: [full_output=True] The derotated cube of residuals.

frame

: Median combination of the de-rotated cube.

classes.name: vip_hci.psfsub.rollsub.ROLL_SUB_Params

Set of parameters for the roll subtraction module.

See function `roll_sub` for documentation.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.__init__
documentation: Frame object initialization.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.crop
documentation:
Cropping the frame.

# Parameters

size

: Size of the subframe.

cenxy

: Coordinates of the center of the subframe.

force

: Size and the size of the 2d array must be both even or odd. With
  `force` set to True this condition can be avoided.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.detect_blobs
documentation: Detecting blobs on the 2d array.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.filter
documentation:
High/low pass filtering the frames of the image.

# Parameters

method

: Low-pass or high-pass filtering.

mode

: Type of low/high-pass filtering.
  `median`

  > [lp] applies a median low-pass filter to the image.

  `gauss`

  : [lp] applies a Gaussian low-pass filter to the image.

  `laplacian`

  : [hp] applies a Laplacian filter with kernel size defined by
    `kernel_size` using the Opencv library.

  `laplacian-conv`

  : [hp] applies a Laplacian high-pass filter by defining a kernel (with
    `kernel_size`) and using the `convolve_fft` Astropy function.

  `median-subt`

  : [hp] subtracts a median low-pass filtered version of the image.

  `gauss-subt`

  : [hp] subtracts a Gaussian low-pass filtered version of the image.

  `fourier-butter`

  : [hp] applies a high-pass 2D Butterworth filter in Fourier domain.

  `hann`

  : [hp] uses a Hann window.

median_size

: Size of the median box for the `median` or `median-subt` filter.

kernel_size

: Size of the Laplacian kernel used in `laplacian` mode. It must be
  a positive odd integer value.

fwhm_size

: Size of the Gaussian kernel used in `gauss` or `gauss-subt` mode.

btw_cutoff

: Frequency cutoff for low-pass 2d Butterworth filter used in
  `fourier-butter` mode.

btw_order

: Order of low-pass 2d Butterworth filter used in `fourier-butter` mode.

hann_cutoff

: Frequency cutoff for the `hann` mode.

gauss_mode

: 'conv' uses the multidimensional gaussian filter from scipy.ndimage and
  'convfft' uses the fft convolution with a 2d Gaussian kernel.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.get_center
documentation:
Getting the coordinates of the center of the image.

Parameters
----------
verbose : bool optional
    If True the center coordinates are printed out.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.plot
documentation:
Plotting the 2d array.

# Parameters

kwargs

: Parameters passed to the function `plot_frames` of the package
  `HCIplot`.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.radial_profile
documentation:
Calculates the average radial profile of an image.

# Parameters

sep

: The average radial profile is recorded every `sep` pixels.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.recenter
documentation:
Recentering the frame using the satellite spots or a radon
transform.

Parameters
----------
method : {'satspots', 'radon'}, str optional
    Method for recentering the frame.
xy : tuple, optional
    Tuple with coordinates X,Y of the satellite spots. When the spots
    are in an X configuration, the order is the following: top-left,
    top-right, bottom-left and bottom-right. When the spots are in an
    + (cross-like) configuration, the order is the following: top,
    right, left, bottom.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.rescale
documentation:
Resampling the image (upscaling or downscaling).

Parameters
----------
scale : int, float or tuple
    Scale factor for upsampling or downsampling the frames in the cube.
    If a tuple it corresponds to the scale along x and y.
imlib : {'ndimage', 'opencv', 'vip-fft'}, str optional
    Library used for image transformations. ndimage is the default.
interpolation : str, optional
    For 'ndimage' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
    'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation
    is the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is
    the worst option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
    For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
    The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
    slowest and accurate.
verbose : bool, optional
    Whether to print out additional info such as the new image shape.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.rotate
documentation:
Rotating the image by a given `angle`.

# Parameters

imlib

: Library used for image transformations. Opencv is faster than
  ndimage or skimage.

interpolation

: For 'skimage' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
  'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation
  is the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is
  the poorer option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
  For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
  The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
  slowest and accurate. 'lanczos4' is the default.

cxy

: Coordinates X,Y of the point with respect to which the rotation
  will be performed. By default the rotation is done with respect to
  the center of the frames, as it is returned by the function
  vip_hci.var.frame_center.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.shift
documentation:
Shifting the image.

Parameters
----------
shift_y, shift_x: float
    Shifts in x and y directions.
imlib : {'opencv', 'ndimage-fourier', 'ndimage-interp', 'vip-fft'}, str opt
    Library or method used for performing the image shift.
    'ndimage-fourier', does a fourier shift operation and preserves
    better the pixel values (therefore the flux and photometry).
    Interpolation based shift ('opencv' and 'ndimage-interp') is faster
    than the fourier shift. 'opencv' is recommended when speed is
    critical.
interpolation : {'bicubic', 'bilinear', 'nearneig'}, optional
    Only used in case of imlib is set to 'opencv' or 'ndimage-interp',
    where the images are shifted via interpolation.
    For 'ndimage-interp' library: 'nearneig', bilinear', 'bicuadratic',
    'bicubic', 'biquartic', 'biquintic'. The 'nearneig' interpolation is
    the fastest and the 'biquintic' the slowest. The 'nearneig' is the
    poorer option for interpolation of noisy astronomical images.
    For 'opencv' library: 'nearneig', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'lanczos4'.
    The 'nearneig' interpolation is the fastest and the 'lanczos4' the
    slowest and accurate. 'lanczos4' is the default.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.snr
documentation:
Calculating the S/N for a test resolution element `source_xy`.

# Parameters

source_xy

: X and Y coordinates of the planet or test speckle.

plot

: Plots the frame and the apertures considered for clarity.

verbose

: Chooses whether to print some output or not.

# Returns

snr_val

: Value of the S/N for `source_xy`.

methods.name: vip_hci.hci_dataset.Frame.stats
documentation:
Calculating statistics on the image, both in the full-frame and in
a region (circular aperture or annulus). Also, the S/N of the either
`source_xy` or the max pixel is calculated.

# Parameters

region

: Region in which basic statistics (mean, stddev, median and max) are
  calculated.

radius

: Radius of the circular aperture.

xy

: Center of the circular aperture.

annulus_inner_radius

: Inner radius of the annular region.

annulus_width

: Width of the annular region.

source_xy

: Coordinates for which the S/N information will be obtained. If None,
  the S/N is estimated for the pixel with the maximum value.

verbose

: Whether to print out the values of the calculated statistics.

plot

: Whether to plot the frame, histograms and region.

High-contrast imaging frame (2d array).

# Parameters

data

: 2d array.

hdu

: If `cube` is a String, `hdu` indicates the HDU from the FITS file.
  By default the first HDU is used.

fwhm

: The FWHM associated with this dataset (instrument dependent). Required
  for several methods (operations on the cube).
